flashcards.back: The set of addresses that a process can use to reference memory, which is separate from physical memory addresses.
The wasted memory within an allocated memory block when the process uses less space than was allocated.
The phenomenon where free memory is broken into small, non-contiguous fragments that cannot be used for new processes despite their cumulative size being sufficient.

flashcards.front: What is the purpose of the Memory Management Unit (MMU)?
flashcards.back: The MMU is a hardware component that translates virtual addresses to physical addresses and provides memory protection.

flashcards.front: What is swapping in memory management?
flashcards.back: The process of moving a process temporarily out of main memory to secondary storage (disk) and then back into memory when needed for execution.
